references.text: Fernando, ES, MO Quimado & AI Doronila. 2014. Rinorea niccolifera (Violaceae), a new, nickel-hyperaccumulating species from Luzon Island, Philippines. Phytokeys 37: 1-13
Flicker, BJ & HR Ballard. 2019. Afrohybanthus (Violaceae), a new genus for a distinctive and widely distributed Old World hybanthoid lineage. Phytotaxa 230: 39-53
Jacobs, M. & DM Moore. 1971. Violaceae. Flora Malesiana series 1, 7: 179-212

Romeroso, RB, DN Tandang & IA Navarrete. 2021. New distributional record of Phyllanthus securinegoides Merr. (Phyllanthaceae) and Rinorea niccolifera Fernando (Violaceae) of Homonhon Island, Philippines. Biodiversitas 22: 499-503

genera.last_edited: Last edited by Pieter B. Pelser, 3 August 2019

species.citation: (L.) Flicker , Phytotaxa 230 (2015) 47; ≡ Hybanthus enneaspermus (L.) F.Muell. , Fragm. (Mueller) 10 (1876) 81; ≡ Ionidium enneaspermum (L.) Vent. , Jard. Malmaison (1803) t.27 ; Merrill, EPFP 3 (1923) 106; ≡ Viola enneasperma L. , Sp. Pl., 1st Ed. (1753) 937.
species.distribution: Africa, Australia, Bangladesh, Borneo, ?Cambodia, China, India, Indian Ocean, Java, Lesser Sunda Isls, Middle East, Moluccas, New Guinea, Philippines, Sri Lanka, Taiwan, Thailand, Vietnam. AMBIL, GOLO, LUZON: Ilocos Norte, MINDANAO: Cotabato.

genera.last_edited: Last edited by Pieter B. Pelser, 16 June 2024

species.citation: (Wall.) Kuntze , Revis. Gen. Pl. 1 (1891) 42 ; Fernando et al., Forest Flora of Dinagat (2017) 91; ≡ Alsodeia bengalensis Wall. , Trans. Med. Soc. Calcutta 7 (1835) 224. = Rinorea fasciculata (Turcz.) Merr. , PJS 12c (1917) 286 ; Merrill, EPFP 3 (1923) 104; ≡ Alsodeia fasciculata (Turcz.) Turcz. , Bull. Soc. Imp. Naturalistes Moscou 36 (1863) 559; ≡ Pentaloba fasciculata Turcz. , Bull. Soc. Imp. Naturalistes Moscou 27 (1854) 342. = Rinorea glandulosa (Elmer) Merr. , PJS 12c (1917) 286 ; Merrill, EPFP 3 (1923) 104; ≡ Gelonium glandulosum Elmer , LPB 3 (1910) 918; Type: Elmer 12315, Sibuyan: Mt Giting-Giting, Apr-1910. = Gelonium trifidum Elmer , LPB 3 (1910) 918.
species.distribution: Andaman Isls, Australia, Bangladesh, Borneo, Cambodia, China, India, Java, Laos, Lesser Sunda Isls, Malay Peninsula, Moluccas, Myanmar, New Guinea, Nicobar Isls, Pacific Ocean, Philippines, Solomon Isls, Sri Lanka, Sulawesi, Sumatra, Thailand, Vietnam. CEBU, DINAGAT, LUZON: Albay, Camarines, Isabela, Laguna, Quezon, Zambales, MINDANAO: Bukidnon, Zamboanga, MINDORO, PALAWAN, PANAY, SAMAR, SIBUYAN.

species.citation: (Korth.) Kuntze , Revis. Gen. Pl. 1 (1891) 42; ≡ Alsodeia horneri Korth. , Ned. Kruidk. Arch. 1 (1848) 360. = Rinorea acuminata Merr. , PJS 5c (1910) 201 ; Merrill, EPFP 3 (1923) 103. = Rinorea copelandii Merr. , Publ. Bur. Sci. Gov. Lab. 35 (1906) 45 ; Merrill, EPFP 3 (1923) 103.
species.distribution: Borneo, Malay Peninsula, Moluccas, New Guinea, Philippines, Solomon Isls, Sulawesi, Sumatra, Thailand. BASILAN, CEBU, MINDANAO: Davao, MINDORO, PALAWAN, TAWI-TAWI.

species.citation: (Blume) Kuntze , Revis. Gen. Pl. 1 (1891) 42; ≡ Alsodeia javanica (Blume) Miq. , Fl. Ned. Ind. 1(2) (1859) 117; ≡ Prosthesia javanica Blume , Bijdr. fl. Ned. Ind. (1826) 867. = Rinorea formicaria (Elmer) Merr. , PJS 12c (1917) 286 ; Merrill, EPFP 3 (1923) 104; ≡ Alsodeia formicaria Elmer , LPB 5 (1913) 1850; Type: Elmer 12886 (A, BM, K, NSW, P, U, US), Palawan: Mt Pulgar, Mar-1911.
species.distribution: Indochina, Java, Malaysia, Philippines, Sulawesi, Sumatra, Thailand. PALAWAN.

species.citation: (Decne.) Kuntze , Revis. Gen. Pl. 1 (1891) 42; ≡ Alsodeia macrophylla Decne. , Nouv. Ann. Mus. Hist. Nat. 3 (1834) 428. = Rinorea palawanensis Merr. , PJS 1(Suppl.) (1906) 210 ; Merrill, EPFP 3 (1923) 104; Type: BS 252 Bermejos (K, US), Palawan, Dec-1905. = Rinorea palawanensis var. mollis Merr. , EPFP 3 (1923) 104.
species.distribution: Indochina, Java, Lesser Sunda Isls, Malay Peninsula, Philippines, Sumatra, Thailand. CEBU, CULION, GUIMARAS, JOLO, PALAWAN, SULU ARCHIPELAGO.

species.citation: Fernando , PhytoKeys 37 (2014) 4 ; Romeroso et al., Biodiversitas 22 (2021) 501; Romeroso et al., J. Asia-Pacific biodiv. 17 (2024) 13; Type: Fernando 3016 (LBC, holo; CAHUP, K, PNH, SING, iso), Luzon: Zambales, Sta. Cruz, Lucapon, 330m, 1-Apr-2012.
species.distribution: Endemic to the Philippines. BUCAS GRANDE (photos), DINAGAT, HOMONHON, LUZON: Zambales, MINDANAO: Surigao del Norte, SAMAR.

genera.last_edited: Last edited by Pieter B. Pelser, 3 May 2026

species.citation: Blume , Bijdr. fl. Ned. Ind. (1825) 58 ; Merrill, EPFP 3 (1923) 104. = Viola semilunaris var. philippinarum W.Becker , Beih. Bot. Centralbl., 2nd Abt. 34 (1916) 231 ; Merrill, EPFP 3 (1923) 106. = Viola toppingii Elmer , LPB 2 (1908) 504.
species.distribution: China, India, Indochina, Java, Moluccas, Myanmar, New Guinea, Philippines, Sumatra. LUZON: Benguet, Nueva Vizcaya (photos).

species.citation: Sm. , Cycl. 37 (1817) n.7. ssp. betonicifolia. = Viola betonicifolia ssp. napaulensis (Ging.) W.Becker, Beibl. Bot. Jahrb. Syst. 120 (1917) 120; Merrill, EPFP 3 (1923) 105, as 'nepalensis'; ≡ Viola patrinii var. napaulensis Ging., Prodr. (A. P. de Candolle) 1 (1824) 293. Notes: Distribution as the species. Native. ssp. nova-guineensis D.M.Moore, Feddes Repert. Spec. Nov. Regni Veg. 68 (1963) 82. Distribution: Lesser Sunda Isls, New Guinea, Philippines, Sulawesi, Sumatra. MINDANAO: Bukidnon. Native.
species.distribution: Australia, China, Himalayas, India, Indochina, Japan, Java, Lesser Sunda Isls, New Guinea, Philippines, Sri Lanka, Sulawesi, Sumatra. LUZON: Benguet, Cagayan, Mountain Province, MINDANAO: Bukidnon, MINDORO.

species.citation: Ging. , Prodr. (A. P. de Candolle) 1 (1824) 293 ; Merrill, EPFP 3 (1923) 105. ssp. diffusa. = Viola diffusa ssp. tenuis (Benth.) W.Becker, PJS 19 (1921) 714; Merrill, EPFP 3 (1923) 105; ≡ Viola tenuis Benth., London J. Bot. 1 (1842) 482. Distribution: China, Himalayas, Indochina, Japan, Philippines, Taiwan. LUZON: Mountain Province. Native. ssp. apoensis (Elmer) D.M.Moore, Fl. Males., 1st Ser. 7 (1971) 202; ≡ Viola apoensis Elmer, LPB 8 (1915) 2878; Merrill, EPFP 3 (1923) 105. Distribution: New Guinea, Philippines. LUZON: Mountain Province, MINDANAO: Davao, Davao del Sur. Native.
species.distribution: China, Himalayas, Indochina, Japan, New Guinea, Philippines, Taiwan. LUZON: Benguet, Mountain Province, Quezon (photos), MINDANAO: Cotabato (photos), Davao, Davao del Sur, MINDORO.

species.citation: Blume , Cat. Gew. Buitenzorg (1823) 57 ; Merrill, EPFP 3 (1923) 105.
species.distribution: China, Himalayas, Java, Malay Peninsula, Myanmar, Philippines, Sumatra. LUZON: Benguet, Cagayan, Ifugao.
Philippines, Sulawesi. LUZON: Mountain Province, MINDANAO: Davao del Sur, Misamis Occidental, NEGROS.

species.citation: W.Becker , Beih. Bot. Centralbl., 2nd Abt. 34 (1916) 234 ; Merrill, EPFP 3 (1923) 105.
species.distribution: Endemic to the Philippines. LUZON: Apayao (photos), Benguet, Mountain Province.

species.citation: Cav. , Icon. (Cavanilles) 6 (1800) 19. = Viola edanoi W.Becker , PJS 19 (1921) 722 ; Merrill, EPFP 3 (1923) 105.
species.distribution: China, Himalayas, India, Japan, Java, Korea, Lesser Sunda Isls, Mongolia, Myanmar, Philippines, Sulawesi, Sumatra. LUZON: Benguet, Kalinga, Mountain Province, MINDANAO: Bukidnon.
Philippines, Taiwan. LUZON: Benguet, Ifugao, Mountain Province, Nueva Vizcaya.

species.notes: Note by L.L.Co: Holotype lost. Isotype in US not agreeing with the original description and may be referable to Viola philippica which also occurs in the type locality of BS 37548.
